title: About Weaver and Book
body: Weaver and Book is an independent literary bookshop at Svartbacksgatan 12, Uppsala, Sweden. Founded by Lucia Weaver, the shop specialises in literary fiction in English, Swedish, and German, with a small French shelf.

title: Contact Us
body: Find us at Svartbäcksgatan 12, 753 32 Uppsala. Open Tuesday to Saturday 10:00-18:00. Closed Sunday and Monday. Email: lucia.weaver@Finthesiss.ai. Phone: +46 18 470 1322.

title: Staff Picks
body: Our curated selection of favourite books chosen by Lucia and Linnea. Updated monthly with new recommendations across all our shelves.

title: Events at Weaver and Book
body: We host author readings, literary discussions, and book club evenings throughout the year. All events take place in the shop unless otherwise noted.

title: The Weaver and Book Book Club
body: Join our monthly book club, meeting on the first Thursday of each month at 18:30. Open to all readers. Current read announced on Instagram.

title: Gift Cards
body: Give the gift of reading with a Weaver and Book gift card. Available in denominations of 200, 300, 500, and 1000 kr. Valid in-store and online.

title: Author: Marta Kowalska
body: Marta Kowalska is a Polish-Swedish author whose work explores diaspora, memory, and the body. Her debut novel The Amber Hours was shortlisted for the Nordic Council Literature Prize.

title: Author: Anders Lilja
body: Anders Lilja is a Swedish poet and novelist from Norrbotten. His debut collection Ljusbrytning was praised for its spare, luminous verse about landscape and loss.

title: Author: Nora Clement
body: Nora Clement is a British novelist and essayist whose work bridges the English and Nordic literary traditions. She lives part of the year in Uppsala.

title: Author: Sofia Nyman
body: Sofia Nyman is one of Sweden's most celebrated contemporary novelists. Skogens Vittnen won the August Prize and has been translated into fourteen languages.

title: Author Reading: Marta Kowalska
body: Join us on 15 November 2026 for an evening with Marta Kowalska, reading from her forthcoming novel. Q and A to follow. Free entry, reservation recommended.

title: Nordic Fiction Evening
body: A panel discussion on contemporary Nordic literature featuring three authors and a literary critic. Tickets 50 kr, includes wine. 10 December 2026 at 18:30.

title: Staff Pick: The Amber Hours
body: Linnea writes: A book I pressed into every customer's hands this spring. Kowalska's prose is both spare and devastating, and the structure rewards careful re-reading.

title: Staff Pick: Skogens Vittnen
body: Lucia writes: The novel that convinced me to open a bookshop. Sofia Nyman writes about Sweden in a way that feels completely new and utterly familiar.

title: Staff Pick: The Salt Psalms
body: Linnea writes: Marta Kowalska's poetry collection is unlike anything else on our shelves. The bilingual format is a reading experience in itself.

title: The Swedish Shelf
body: Our Swedish-language fiction and poetry section is curated with care, from canonical authors to exciting debuts. Ask Linnea for a recommendation.

title: The German Shelf
body: We stock a selected range of German-language literary fiction and poetry, with a focus on contemporary authors published by Albatross Verlag and others.

title: The Small French Shelf
body: A curated selection of French-language literature occupies one shelf near the window. We rotate stock seasonally and welcome special orders.

title: Shipping and Delivery
body: We ship across Sweden and internationally. Swedish orders dispatched within two working days. International shipping times vary. Free shipping on Swedish orders over 500 kr.

title: Returns and Exchanges
body: Unopened books in original condition may be returned within 14 days of purchase for a full refund or exchange. Gift cards are non-refundable.

title: Subscribe to Our Newsletter
body: Sign up for the Weaver and Book newsletter for monthly staff picks, event announcements, and reading recommendations delivered to your inbox.

title: The Weaver and Book Blog
body: Occasional essays and reflections from Lucia and Linnea on books, bookselling, and Uppsala literary life. New posts every few weeks.
